mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values indicate inflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that erode profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to positive during tightening periods or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, immediately boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of rate-cut observations or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with stable unemployment rates indicate healthy job creation that sustains consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Combined labor strength signals yield negative 60-day returns in >55% of instances or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annually signals economic stagnation that depresses corporate revenue expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns remain positive in >65% of low-growth quarters or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization that lifts equity prices, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of recovery-phase observations or loses predictive power outside the specified regime.
